# Story 5.4: Post-publish verification and cross-platform install smoke test

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

> **Precondition summary (2026-04-24 create-story time):** v1.0.0 is live on npm under `--tag latest` with SLSA L2 provenance — published `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` (Story 5.3). `main` tip is [`55f40d0`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/55f40d0) — PR #215 merge (Story 5.3 code-review patches). `jq -r .version package.json` on `main` returns `1.0.0`; `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` returns `1.0.0`; CHANGELOG has a single reconciled `## [1.0.0]` block; `git tag -l 'v1.0.0'` returns `v1.0.0` (annotated, anchored on PR #213 merge commit `393a5a2`). npm dist-tags: `{"latest":"1.0.0","alpha":"0.10.1-alpha.0","rc":"1.0.0-rc.3"}`. `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut § Decision: v1.0.0 launch status` reads `LAUNCHED — v1.0.0 is live on npm under --tag latest with SLSA L2 provenance. Story 5.4 cross-platform verification cleared to begin (NFR9: within 1h of publish).` Sprint-status: 5-4 is `backlog`; after create-story it flips to `ready-for-dev`; Epic 5 is `in-progress` (flips to `done` when 5-4 reaches `done`).
>
> **NFR9 clock state at create-story time:** the 1-hour publish window (`2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` → `2026-04-23T19:56:39Z`) has elapsed. Story 5.4 is being authored `2026-04-24`, i.e. **after** the NFR9 1-hour target. This does NOT cancel the verification — the cross-platform smoke test is still the required v1.0.0 launch gate and feeds the same audit artifact — but the elapsed-time record will document a missed 1-hour target. See Dev Notes (B) for the disposition protocol (record the actual elapsed-time bucket, mark NFR9 status as `missed-target-non-blocking`, triage whether any cross-platform regression existed during the window we were blind to).
>
> **Relationship to Stories 5.1 / 5.2 / 5.3 / 6.x:** Story 5.1 gated Epic 5 via the pre-flight audit. Story 5.2 cut `v1.0.0-rc.3` and smoke-tested it interactively (Linux only). Story 5.3 promoted to `v1.0.0` under `--tag latest` and activated NFR6 immutability. Story 5.4 (this story) runs the cross-platform post-publish smoke test satisfying NFR9. Once Story 5.4 closes with PASS, Epic 5 reaches `done` and Epic 6 cleanup (6-1 / 6-2 / 6-3) is unblocked.

## Story

As the maintainer (and on behalf of downstream users),
I want `v1.0.0` install verified across the supported platform matrix via the published npm tarball,
so that any platform-specific regression is caught and classified (blocking vs non-blocking) with enough lead-time to issue `npm deprecate` + ship `v1.0.1` while the blast radius remains small.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Scope envelope — two commits total in the happy path.** (a) **Commit 1: post-publish smoke-test workflow + NFR9 cross-reference.** Subject `feat(release): add post-publish install smoke workflow (Story 5.4)`. Touches EXACTLY two files: `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` (NEW file — permanent reusable dispatch workflow per AC 3) AND `docs/RELEASING.md` (MODIFIED — append an NFR9 cross-reference block to the `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest` section authored by Story 5.3, linking the smoke workflow as the post-publish verification step; per `deferred-work.md § S53-D6`). (b) **Commit 2: verification transcripts + audit sign-off.** Subject `docs(release): record cross-platform v1.0.0 install verification (Story 5.4)`. Touches EXACTLY one file: `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` (append `## Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification` H2 at EOF; update the top-level `## Sign-off` block to append a third sentence per AC 9). **Verification:** `git log main --grep='Story 5.4' --oneline` returns both commit subjects; `git log main --grep='install-smoke.yaml' --name-only` returns a single `A .github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` addition.

2. **NFR9 clock-window documentation — record the actual elapsed time from publish to verification.** The target is 60 min (`2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` publish → `2026-04-23T19:56:39Z` deadline). Story 5.4 is authored 2026-04-24 (post-deadline). The audit artifact (AC 8 subsection `### NFR9 elapsed-time record`) SHALL compute the wall-clock delta from publish to smoke-workflow completion (in hours:minutes), classify the NFR9 status as one of `within-window | missed-target-non-blocking | missed-target-with-live-defect` per AC 7 triage outcome, and record the actual timestamps. A missed NFR9 window when no live cross-platform defect was present is **non-blocking** for Epic 5 closure (the policy's safety goal — catching a defect fast while blast radius is small — is achieved in practice, just later than the 1-hour target). This disposition SHALL be explicit in the audit so the Epic 5 retrospective can weigh whether the 1-hour target needs softening, automation (e.g., post-publish workflow auto-triggered by tag push), or staffing commitments.

3. **`.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` — new permanent workflow authored in Commit 1.** Shape SHALL be:
   - `name: Install Smoke Test`
   - `on: workflow_dispatch` with a `version` input (string, default `"latest"`, description pointing at npm dist-tags like `latest`/`rc`/`alpha` or explicit versions like `1.0.0`)
   - `permissions: { contents: read }` (no write — smoke test only fetches from npm)
   - Single job `smoke` with matrix strategy `{ os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest], fail-fast: false }`
   - `runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}`
   - Steps: (i) `actions/setup-node@v4` with `node-version: '22'` (matching `.nvmrc`; no checkout needed — the smoke test is against the published npm package, not the repo source), (ii) `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@${{ inputs.version }} --version` with explicit `if-no-files-found`/`exit 0` assertion in a shell-portable one-liner
   - NO additional steps (`install` subcommand is interactive-only per Story 5.2 AC 9 deviation note — issue #211; `status` requires prior install state so is not CI-runnable without first driving the interactive install). `--version` is the maximum-portable smoke check: it validates the tarball resolves, the `bin` wrapper (`tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js`) executes, commander dispatch reaches `tools/cli/skf-cli.js`, and the version string from `package.json` is readable and printed — the common cross-platform regression vectors (tarball corruption, shebang issues, platform-specific bin wrapper bugs, filesystem case-sensitivity).
   - **Adding macOS to the matrix goes BEYOND NFR9's "manual spot-check" — NFR9 allows manual macOS but doesn't forbid CI automation.** Including `macos-latest` converts the manual-spot-check obligation into a reproducible CI artifact and reduces the maintenance burden for every future release. The matrix triplet `[ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]` matches the npm public-repo Actions free-tier coverage and the `Dev Notes § (C)` rationale.

4. **Ubuntu smoke test run — first mandatory dispatch.** After Commit 1 lands on `main`: dispatch `install-smoke.yaml` with `version: latest`. The ubuntu-latest job SHALL:
   - Resolve `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@latest` to `1.0.0` (observable in the Actions log; the `--yes` flag suppresses the interactive confirm for first-install of a package)
   - Print `1.0.0` to stdout (the declared version in `package.json.version`)
   - Exit `0` (no stderr noise, no permission errors, no shebang failures)
   - Complete in under 60 seconds typical wall-clock
   
   **Blocker criterion:** if the ubuntu job fails, HALT Commit 2 — capture the failing-step log, triage per AC 7, decide blocking vs non-blocking. A ubuntu failure is a CRITICAL blocker (majority of downstream installs are Linux) and triggers the Epic 4 Rollback Playbook Scenario B (`npm deprecate v1.0.0 "<reason>"` + prepare v1.0.1).

5. **Windows smoke test run — same dispatch, matrix sibling.** The windows-latest job of the same dispatch SHALL meet the same success criteria as AC 4. Windows is the #1 platform-divergence risk surface for SKF per `quality.yaml:76-78` comment (path handling, line endings, shell scripts) — any failure here is likely to expose a latent Windows bug in the bin wrapper or commander dispatch. Blocker criterion: same as AC 4 (Windows install failure → HALT Commit 2, triage, likely Scenario B rollback).

6. **macOS smoke test run — same dispatch, matrix sibling (CI-automated coverage of NFR9's manual-spot-check obligation).** The macos-latest job of the same dispatch SHALL meet the same success criteria as AC 4 & AC 5. **NFR9 allows manual spot-check for macOS; Story 5.4 elects to include macOS in the CI matrix instead.** This elevates the spot-check guarantee from "best-effort maintainer run" to "CI-reproducible artifact." **Fallback:** if `macos-latest` becomes unavailable or costs increase, the job can be moved to manual spot-check per NFR9's text; this falls back safely without violating the NFR. Blocker criterion: macOS failure is a BLOCKING HIGH (smaller macOS user base but a silent macOS regression would be a bad launch story). Triage per AC 7.

7. **Triage protocol for any failure — blocking vs non-blocking classification.** If any matrix leg of the smoke workflow fails:
   - (a) Capture the failing step, full log, and `package.json`/shebang/`bin`-wrapper state from the failure context
   - (b) Reproduce locally if possible (maintainer has Linux access — Ubuntu WSL counts; Windows + macOS reproduction is best-effort unless maintainer has hardware)
   - (c) Classify:
     - **BLOCKING:** install cannot complete on a supported platform at all (exit !=0 for `--version`, tarball corrupt, bin wrapper missing, shebang platform-incompatible). This triggers `npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 "<specific reason>"` IMMEDIATELY per `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook § Scenario B`, followed by `release.yaml -f version_bump=patch` to cut `v1.0.1` with the platform fix. Epic 5 does NOT close until `v1.0.1` ships clean.
     - **NON-BLOCKING:** install succeeds but a cosmetic/warning artifact appears (stderr noise, deprecation warnings from a transitive dep, slower-than-typical resolution). Record in Commit 2's audit artifact as "NFR9 PASS with observations"; do NOT trigger deprecate.
   - (d) If BLOCKING, file a same-repo GitHub issue titled `v1.0.0 install regression: <platform> <one-line>`. No `_bmad-output/todo/` references. No cross-repo issue refs (per user-memory `feedback_no_issue_numbers_in_pr.md`).

## Dev Notes

### What this story actually is

A **post-publish cross-platform smoke test** that satisfies NFR9 by dispatching a new reusable `install-smoke.yaml` workflow on ubuntu+windows+macos, capturing matrix outputs as verification evidence, and signing off the v1.0.0 launch audit. The story authors **one new permanent workflow** (reusable for every future release) and **two commits** (the workflow + RELEASING cross-reference, then the verification transcripts). No repo-source code changes.

**What this story is NOT:**

- NOT a full interactive install verification — the `install` subcommand's multiselect IDE prompt cannot be driven by piped stdin (issue #211); CI smoke tests only the `--version` path. Full interactive install + `status` + `uninstall` were validated by Story 5.2 rc.3 on Linux; Windows + macOS interactive spot-checks remain maintainer-optional.
- NOT a deprecate or unpublish of v1.0.0 — regardless of any blocking regression discovered, NFR6 forbids unpublish; Scenario B (deprecate + ship v1.0.1) is the only rollback path.
- NOT a code change in `tools/` / `src/` / `test/` / `docs/STABILITY.md` — v1.0.0 is the artifact under verification, not under amendment.
- NOT an edit to `release.yaml` — the canonical release workflow is untouched; only `install-smoke.yaml` (new) and `docs/RELEASING.md` (NFR9 cross-reference append) change in Commit 1.
- NOT an `npm view`-based attestation re-audit — that was Story 5.3's AC 6 territory; Story 5.4 assumes attestation state as established.
- NOT a release-workflow re-run — no `release.yaml` dispatch happens in Story 5.4; the `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch is the ONLY workflow action.
- NOT a CHANGELOG edit — Story 5.3 reconciled CHANGELOG; Story 5.4 touches only the audit artifact for sign-off.

### Load-bearing decisions already made at create-story (no Dev-Agent re-derivation needed)

**(A) Three-platform CI matrix for smoke, not two + manual.** NFR9 reads "Linux + Windows via quality.yaml matrix within 1 hour post-publish; macOS SHALL be spot-checked manually." We elect to include `macos-latest` in the smoke matrix. Rationale: (i) it's free on public repos under the standard Actions runner allocation; (ii) it converts a soft manual-spot-check into a reproducible CI artifact, with the same evidence surface (Actions run URL + logs) as Linux + Windows; (iii) future releases inherit automatic macOS coverage without requiring maintainer hardware availability; (iv) NFR9 permits this (explicit allowance of manual doesn't forbid automation). Fallback if `macos-latest` becomes scarce or costs change post-v1: remove from matrix, revert to manual per NFR9 literal.

**(B) NFR9 1-hour window was missed at create-story time.** Story 5.3 published at `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`; the 1-hour NFR9 window closed at `2026-04-23T19:56:39Z`. Story 5.4 is authored `2026-04-24` — well past the deadline. The story proceeds with the full verification regardless: the purpose of NFR9 (catch cross-platform regressions while blast radius is small) is still served by running the smoke test as soon as practical. The audit (AC 8 `### NFR9 elapsed-time record`) SHALL document the actual elapsed time and classify the miss per AC 2. This is the FIRST NFR9 data point; Epic 5 retrospective will decide whether to: (i) keep the 1-hour target and add automation to trigger smoke on tag-push (converting NFR9 to a near-real-time auto-verification); (ii) soften the target to 24 hours for solo-maintainer operability; (iii) leave the target and commit to on-call staffing for future launches. None of those is a Story 5.4 decision — Story 5.4 records the miss and lets the retro decide policy.

**(C) `--version` is the maximum-portable smoke check.** The full CLI surface exposes `install` (interactive, multiselect-required, not pipe-able per Story 5.2 rc.3), `status` (non-interactive but requires prior install state — would fail in a clean CI runner), `uninstall` (pipe-able via `printf 'y\n'` but same prior-install dependency as `status`), `update` (interactive, same pipe-ability issues as install), and `--version` / `--help` (non-interactive, no dependencies, maximally portable). `--version` is chosen over `--help` because it asserts that `package.json.version` is correctly readable at runtime (touches the bin wrapper → commander → version-flag wiring) — `--help` would only exercise commander's help registry without touching version-read. For every platform the smoke runs on, a successful `--version` proves: (i) npm tarball resolves and extracts cleanly; (ii) `bin` wrapper (`tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js`) is executable with the correct shebang for the platform; (iii) Node can load and execute the CLI entrypoint; (iv) commander dispatch is functional; (v) `package.json` is readable at runtime. This covers every common cross-platform regression vector without requiring TTY simulation.

**(D) Two-commit envelope over single-commit.** A single-commit envelope (workflow + audit append together) would leave the smoke-workflow's dispatch unobserved at commit time — the audit transcript would be authored before the workflow runs. Two commits enforce temporal ordering: (i) Commit 1 lands the workflow, (ii) a fresh dispatch captures ground-truth matrix evidence, (iii) Commit 2 records that evidence. This matches Story 5.2 & 5.3's two-commit patterns and makes the audit authentically post-evidence.

**(E) No repo checkout in the smoke workflow.** The smoke test runs against the PUBLISHED npm tarball, not the repo source. Checking out `main` would pollute the runner with repo files and delay each job by ~5-10s (clone + cache restore). Using `actions/setup-node@v4` with explicit `node-version: '22'` (hardcoded to match `.nvmrc`) avoids checkout entirely. Trade-off: if `.nvmrc` ever advances to Node 23+, the smoke workflow's hardcoded `22` becomes stale — a dedicated audit-item to update the workflow in lockstep with any future Node-major bump. Dev-agent adds a comment in the workflow file noting this coupling.

**(F) `fail-fast: false` on the matrix.** If ubuntu fails, we still want windows and macos to run so the full matrix picture is visible in one dispatch. `fail-fast: true` would cancel siblings on first failure and force re-dispatch per-platform, adding latency to triage. This mirrors `quality.yaml`'s matrix pattern (`fail-fast: false` at lines 81 and 129).

**(G) `permissions: contents: read` is the minimum — no write, no id-token.** The smoke workflow fetches from npm (public), executes node, and exits. It does NOT need `contents: write` (doesn't push), `id-token: write` (doesn't publish), or `packages:` (doesn't read GH Packages). Minimum-privilege per npm trusted-publishing migration ethos (Story 1.3 + 3.1 + NFR7).

**(H) `install-smoke.yaml` becomes a permanent reusable artifact.** Every future release cut (`v1.0.1`, `v1.1.0`, `v2.0.0`, …) can redispatch this workflow with the new version tag to produce the same smoke evidence. This is the first durable cross-platform CI artifact in SKF's release infrastructure — prior smoke tests were ad-hoc maintainer runs (Story 5.2 rc.3). Story 5.4 is investing in the tooling, not just running a one-shot check.

**(I) NFR6 firewall on ANY smoke failure.** The v1.0.0 version is immutable from `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` onwards. A smoke failure NEVER justifies `npm unpublish bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0` regardless of the npm 72-hour eligibility window. The only forward path is Scenario B: `npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 "<reason>"` + `release.yaml -f version_bump=patch` to cut `v1.0.1`. AC 15 encodes this.

**(J) Zero RC-style iteration loop.** Unlike Story 5.2's 7-attempt saga (pipeline defect discovery) and Story 5.3's clean first-attempt success (pipeline E2E-proven), Story 5.4 has ONE dispatch expected: `install-smoke.yaml` with `version: latest`. If that dispatch fails with a BLOCKING defect, the response is Scenario B ship-forward (outside Story 5.4 scope), NOT re-dispatch of the smoke workflow. If it fails with a NON-BLOCKING defect (cosmetic warnings), it still counts as PASS — record observations in audit.

# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

file.content:
- name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: markdownlint
        run: npm run lint:md

  validate:
    # Matrix Linux + Windows to catch platform-divergence bugs (path handling,
    # line endings, shell scripts). macOS skipped: POSIX-equivalent to Linux
    # for SKF's Node + Python surface area — add only if a Mac-only bug surfaces.
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Smoke test conventional-changelog-cli bin resolution on Windows
        if: runner.os == 'Windows'
        run: npx --no-install conventional-changelog-cli --help

      - name: Validate YAML schemas
        run: npm run validate:schemas

      - name: Run agent schema validation tests
        run: npm run test:schemas

      - name: Test installation components
        run: npm run test:install

      - name: Test CLI integration
        run: npm run test:cli

      - name: Test workflow state consistency
        run: npm run test:workflow

      - name: Test knowledge base
        run: npm run test:knowledge

      - name: Validate skills structure
        run: npm run validate:skills

      - name: Validate file references
        run: npm run validate:refs

  python:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:

content.content: 559:### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest

559	### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest
560	
561	- **Trigger.** The passing RC has survived a clean-environment smoke test (`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.2 RC Cut + Smoke Test § Decision: PASS — cleared for Story 5.3 promotion to v1.0.0 under --tag latest with manual approval`); `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@rc version` returns the passing RC (at Story 5.3 dispatch time: `1.0.0-rc.3`); Story 5.3 is ready to promote to `latest` under manual approval.
562	- **CLI.**
563	
564	  ```bash
565	  git checkout main && git pull
566	
567	  # Pre-dispatch sanity check (read version from remote, not local clone).
568	  gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/package.json \
569	    --jq '.content' | base64 -d | jq -r .version
570	  # expected: the passing RC (e.g., 1.0.0-rc.3)
571	
572	  # Dispatch the canonical release workflow. `version_bump: major` on
573	  # 1.0.0-rc.N strips the prerelease suffix and produces 1.0.0 (node-semver
574	  # inc(ver, 'major') behavior on a prerelease). `--ref main` is explicit
575	  # (gh defaults to the repo's default branch, but any non-main ref takes
576	  # the legacy tag-only path inside release.yaml, so pass it defensively).
577	  gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=major --ref main
578	
579	  # Capture the run id for monitoring + audit transcript.
580	  RUN_ID=$(gh run list --workflow=release.yaml --limit 1 \
581	    --json databaseId --jq '.[0].databaseId')
582	  gh run watch $RUN_ID
583	  ```
584	
585	  The workflow pauses at **two** gates that the maintainer must clear in the browser. Gate 1 requires explicit approval; gate 2 accepts either approval or admin-bypass-merge:
586	
587	  1. The `release` environment deployment gate (at job start). Approve via "Review deployments" → "Approve and deploy" on the run page.
588	  2. The bot PR review-decision gate (after the 7 required status checks pass). EITHER approve the bot PR via the review UI, OR admin-bypass-merge via the PR merge button — both paths are accepted by `release.yaml`'s `Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge` step. Admin-bypass-merge is the observed pattern for prior cuts (PRs #209 and #213).
589	
590	  Expected wall-clock: ~5–8 minutes end-to-end when both gates are approved promptly.
591	
592	- **Expected outcome.** `main` tip advances by 2 commits (the `release: bump to v1.0.0` commit on the bot temp branch `release/bot/v1.0.0-<run_id>`, plus the merge commit from the auto-merged or admin-bypass-merged bot PR); `jq -r .version package.json` → `1.0.0`; `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` → `1.0.0`; `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags.latest` → `1.0.0` (flipped from the prior stable, e.g. `0.10.0`); `rc` dist-tag UNCHANGED at the prior RC; SLSA L2 provenance attached (NFR4); GitHub Release `v1.0.0` with `prerelease: false` (NFR6 threshold — `1.0.0` contains no `alpha|beta|rc` substring); tag `v1.0.0` anchors on the bot PR's merge commit per Story 3.4 P9 (the tag is annotated, so `git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}'` gives the commit SHA to compare against `main` tip).
593	
594	- **Constraints.**
595	  - Execute ONLY after the RC audit `§ Sign-off` is populated AND the smoke-test `Decision` is `PASS`.
596	  - The cut is IRREVERSIBLE — once `npm publish --tag latest` succeeds for `1.0.0`, NFR6 forbids unpublish regardless of eligibility window. Rollback is `npm deprecate` + ship-forward (Scenarios A / B).
597	  - The CHANGELOG reconciliation is a POST-workflow step (authored as a separate sign-off commit on `feat/v1-final-signoff`). Do NOT hand-edit the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder BEFORE dispatch — let the workflow auto-generate its `## [1.0.0]` block (which may be EMPTY when there are zero conventional-commit `feat:` / `fix:` entries between the RC and the final cut, which is the normal case), then merge the hand-curated prose into the auto-gen header's position and delete the stale placeholder in one commit.
598	  - Do NOT pre-bump `package.json` or `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` — the workflow's `Bump version` and `Update marketplace.json version` steps handle both atomically and own those files.
599	  - If `npm version major` unexpectedly emits `2.0.0` instead of `1.0.0` on `1.0.0-rc.N`, the node-semver engine behavior has regressed. Abort the dispatch and investigate before retry — a workflow override (`npm version 1.0.0 --no-git-tag-version`) would be required in `release.yaml`.
600	
601	- **Verification.**
602	
603	  ```bash
604	  # Confirm the latest dist-tag flipped to 1.0.0.
605	  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json
606	  # expected: {"latest":"1.0.0","alpha":"...","rc":"1.0.0-rc.N"}
607	
608	  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@latest version
609	  # expected: 1.0.0
610	
611	  # Confirm SLSA L2 provenance (NFR4 CRITICAL). A null attestation is a
612	  # blocker — emergency-deprecate + cut 1.0.1 with provenance per Scenario B.
613	  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 --json | jq '.dist.attestations'
614	  # expected: non-null object; .provenance.predicateType starts with
615	  #           "https://slsa.dev/provenance/"
616	
617	  # Confirm the GitHub Release exists and is NOT a prerelease (NFR6 threshold).
618	  gh release view v1.0.0 --json tagName,isPrerelease
619	  # expected: {"tagName":"v1.0.0","isPrerelease":false}
620	
621	  # Confirm the v1.0.0 tag is reachable from main (annotated tag — dereference with ^{}).
622	  # Uses --is-ancestor so the check stays valid after subsequent commits land on main;
623	  # pin to the recorded merge SHA if you need strict tag-anchor equivalence.
624	  git fetch origin --tags
625	  git merge-base --is-ancestor "$(git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}')" origin/main && echo OK
626	  # expected: OK
627	
628	  # Confirm CHANGELOG reconciliation after the sign-off commit lands on main.

### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest

- **Trigger.** The passing RC has survived a clean-environment smoke test (`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.2 RC Cut + Smoke Test § Decision: PASS — cleared for Story 5.3 promotion to v1.0.0 under --tag latest with manual approval`); `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@rc version` returns the passing RC (at Story 5.3 dispatch time: `1.0.0-rc.3`); Story 5.3 is ready to promote to `latest` under manual approval.
- **CLI.**

  ```bash
  git checkout main && git pull

  # Pre-dispatch sanity check (read version from remote, not local clone).
  gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/package.json \
    --jq '.content' | base64 -d | jq -r .version
  # expected: the passing RC (e.g., 1.0.0-rc.3)

  # Dispatch the canonical release workflow. `version_bump: major` on
  # 1.0.0-rc.N strips the prerelease suffix and produces 1.0.0 (node-semver
  # inc(ver, 'major') behavior on a prerelease). `--ref main` is explicit
  # (gh defaults to the repo's default branch, but any non-main ref takes
  # the legacy tag-only path inside release.yaml, so pass it defensively).
  gh workflow run release.yaml -f version_bump=major --ref main

  # Capture the run id for monitoring + audit transcript.
  RUN_ID=$(gh run list --workflow=release.yaml --limit 1 \
    --json databaseId --jq '.[0].databaseId')
  gh run watch $RUN_ID
  ```

  The workflow pauses at **two** gates that the maintainer must clear in the browser. Gate 1 requires explicit approval; gate 2 accepts either approval or admin-bypass-merge:

  1. The `release` environment deployment gate (at job start). Approve via "Review deployments" → "Approve and deploy" on the run page.
  2. The bot PR review-decision gate (after the 7 required status checks pass). EITHER approve the bot PR via the review UI, OR admin-bypass-merge via the PR merge button — both paths are accepted by `release.yaml`'s `Wait for PR approval or admin-bypass merge` step. Admin-bypass-merge is the observed pattern for prior cuts (PRs #209 and #213).

  Expected wall-clock: ~5–8 minutes end-to-end when both gates are approved promptly.

- **Expected outcome.** `main` tip advances by 2 commits (the `release: bump to v1.0.0` commit on the bot temp branch `release/bot/v1.0.0-<run_id>`, plus the merge commit from the auto-merged or admin-bypass-merged bot PR); `jq -r .version package.json` → `1.0.0`; `jq -r '.plugins[0].version' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json` → `1.0.0`; `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags.latest` → `1.0.0` (flipped from the prior stable, e.g. `0.10.0`); `rc` dist-tag UNCHANGED at the prior RC; SLSA L2 provenance attached (NFR4); GitHub Release `v1.0.0` with `prerelease: false` (NFR6 threshold — `1.0.0` contains no `alpha|beta|rc` substring); tag `v1.0.0` anchors on the bot PR's merge commit per Story 3.4 P9 (the tag is annotated, so `git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}'` gives the commit SHA to compare against `main` tip).

- **Constraints.**
  - Execute ONLY after the RC audit `§ Sign-off` is populated AND the smoke-test `Decision` is `PASS`.
  - The cut is IRREVERSIBLE — once `npm publish --tag latest` succeeds for `1.0.0`, NFR6 forbids unpublish regardless of eligibility window. Rollback is `npm deprecate` + ship-forward (Scenarios A / B).
  - The CHANGELOG reconciliation is a POST-workflow step (authored as a separate sign-off commit on `feat/v1-final-signoff`). Do NOT hand-edit the `## [1.0.0] - TBD` placeholder BEFORE dispatch — let the workflow auto-generate its `## [1.0.0]` block (which may be EMPTY when there are zero conventional-commit `feat:` / `fix:` entries between the RC and the final cut, which is the normal case), then merge the hand-curated prose into the auto-gen header's position and delete the stale placeholder in one commit.
  - Do NOT pre-bump `package.json` or `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` — the workflow's `Bump version` and `Update marketplace.json version` steps handle both atomically and own those files.
  - If `npm version major` unexpectedly emits `2.0.0` instead of `1.0.0` on `1.0.0-rc.N`, the node-semver engine behavior has regressed. Abort the dispatch and investigate before retry — a workflow override (`npm version 1.0.0 --no-git-tag-version`) would be required in `release.yaml`.

- **Verification.**

  ```bash
  # Confirm the latest dist-tag flipped to 1.0.0.
  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json
  # expected: {"latest":"1.0.0","alpha":"...","rc":"1.0.0-rc.N"}

  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@latest version
  # expected: 1.0.0

  # Confirm SLSA L2 provenance (NFR4 CRITICAL). A null attestation is a
  # blocker — emergency-deprecate + cut 1.0.1 with provenance per Scenario B.
  npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 --json | jq '.dist.attestations'
  # expected: non-null object; .provenance.predicateType starts with
  #           "https://slsa.dev/provenance/"

  # Confirm the GitHub Release exists and is NOT a prerelease (NFR6 threshold).
  gh release view v1.0.0 --json tagName,isPrerelease
  # expected: {"tagName":"v1.0.0","isPrerelease":false}

  # Confirm the v1.0.0 tag is reachable from main (annotated tag — dereference with ^{}).
  # Uses --is-ancestor so the check stays valid after subsequent commits land on main;
  # pin to the recorded merge SHA if you need strict tag-anchor equivalence.
  git fetch origin --tags
  git merge-base --is-ancestor "$(git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}')" origin/main && echo OK
  # expected: OK

  # Confirm CHANGELOG reconciliation after the sign-off commit lands on main.

content.content:
628	  # Confirm CHANGELOG reconciliation after the sign-off commit lands on main.
629	  grep -c '^## \[1\.0\.0\] - TBD' CHANGELOG.md    # expected: 0 (stale placeholder gone)
630	  grep -cE '^## \[1\.0\.0\]' CHANGELOG.md         # expected: 1 (one reconciled block)
631	  grep -cE '^## \[1\.0\.0\]\(https' CHANGELOG.md  # expected: 1 (compare-URL header survived)
632	  ```
633	
634	  **NFR6 immutability activation** is the `npm publish --tag latest` success timestamp for `1.0.0`, recorded verbatim in `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut § NFR6 v1.0.0 immutability activation`. For Story 5.3's dispatch, that instant was **2026-04-23T18:56:39Z**. From that moment, `v1.0.0` is forever-burned — `npm deprecate` + ship-forward is the only rollback path.
635	
636	  The `release` environment + bot PR approval-or-admin-bypass-merge pattern is the canonical flow for all main-dispatched cuts since Story 3.4 ([GitHub issue #198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198), [PR #199](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/199)): the release commit is pushed to a temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>`, a bot PR is opened against `main`, the 7 required status checks are force-triggered against the temp branch via `workflow_dispatch`, and the merge is gated behind maintainer approval at both the `release` environment gate and the PR review-decision gate. Non-main dispatches (feature-branch alpha cuts) skip the PR dance entirely and keep the legacy tag-only behavior.

input.content:
# Post-publish install smoke test (NFR9 cross-platform verification).
#
# Purpose: verify a published `bmad-module-skill-forge` version resolves and
# executes cleanly on ubuntu-latest + windows-latest + macos-latest by running
# `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@<version> --version` on each runner.
#
# Trigger: `workflow_dispatch` — run manually after a release (e.g. `latest`,
# `rc`, `alpha`, or an explicit version like `1.0.0`). NOT coupled to
# `release.yaml`; runs against the published npm tarball, not the repo source.
#
# Expected use: the maintainer dispatches this workflow within 1 hour after a
# cut per NFR9; results feed the matching release audit artifact's
# `## Story <N> Post-Publish Verification § Matrix results` table.
#
# Why `--version` and not `install` / `status`: the interactive `install`
# subcommand multiselect prompt is not pipe-able from CI stdin (issue #211);
# `status` and `uninstall` depend on prior install state. `--version` is the
# maximum-portable smoke check — it proves the npm tarball resolves, the
# `bin` wrapper (`tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js`) is executable with the correct
# shebang, Node can load the CLI entrypoint, commander dispatch works, and
# `package.json.version` is readable at runtime. That covers the common
# cross-platform regression vectors (tarball corruption, shebang issues,
# bin-wrapper bugs, filesystem case-sensitivity) without TTY simulation.
#
# Node version coupling: `node-version: '22'` is hardcoded to match
# `.nvmrc`. If `.nvmrc` advances to Node 23+, update this workflow in
# lockstep.

name: Install Smoke Test

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version:
        description: "npm dist-tag (latest / rc / alpha) or explicit version (e.g. 1.0.0)"
        required: true
        type: string
        default: "latest"

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  smoke:
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Run npx bmad-module-skill-forge --version
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          OUTPUT="$(npx --yes "bmad-module-skill-forge@${{ inputs.version }}" --version)"
          echo "stdout: ${OUTPUT}"
          if [ -z "${OUTPUT}" ]; then
            echo "::error::--version produced empty stdout"
            exit 1
          fi

input.new_string:
The `release` environment + bot PR approval-or-admin-bypass-merge pattern is the canonical flow for all main-dispatched cuts since Story 3.4 ([GitHub issue #198](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/issues/198), [PR #199](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/199)): the release commit is pushed to a temp branch `release/bot/vX.Y.Z-<run_id>`, a bot PR is opened against `main`, the 7 required status checks are force-triggered against the temp branch via `workflow_dispatch`, and the merge is gated behind maintainer approval at both the `release` environment gate and the PR review-decision gate. Non-main dispatches (feature-branch alpha cuts) skip the PR dance entirely and keep the legacy tag-only behavior.

#### Post-publish verification (NFR9)

Cross-platform install verification for any cut is performed by the [`install-smoke.yaml`](../.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml) workflow, not by `release.yaml` itself. Dispatch it within 1 hour of publish per NFR9:

```bash
gh workflow run install-smoke.yaml -f version=latest --ref main
```

The workflow fans a `workflow_dispatch` input over `ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, and `macos-latest`, running `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@<version> --version` on each runner. A clean three-leg run is the canonical post-publish evidence — its run URL + matrix table belong in the release audit artifact's `## Story <N> Post-Publish Verification` section. Any failing leg routes through the `Rollback Playbook § Scenario B` (deprecate + ship `vX.Y.Z+1`).

git -C /home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(release): add post-publish install smoke workflow (Story 5.4)

Authors `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` — a permanent reusable
`workflow_dispatch` workflow that runs `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@<version>
--version` across `ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, and `macos-latest` to satisfy
NFR9's cross-platform install-verification obligation. The workflow has no
checkout step (runs against the published npm tarball), `permissions: contents: read`
only, `fail-fast: false` on the matrix, and a hardcoded `node-version: '22'` matching
`.nvmrc` (coupling noted in a workflow-file comment).

Files:
- `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` (A) — new reusable post-publish smoke workflow
- `docs/RELEASING.md` (M) — appends `#### Post-publish verification (NFR9)` subheading
  under `### Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest`, pointing at install-smoke.yaml as the
  canonical NFR9 verification vehicle (closes deferred-work item S53-D6).

Dispatch (after merge): gh workflow run install-smoke.yaml -f version=latest --ref main
Run URL + 3-leg matrix table will land in release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md
§ Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification per AC 8 of Story 5.4.

Refs: AC 1a, AC 3, AC 10, AC 11, AC 13, AC 14 (zero repo-source changes).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --head feat/v1-install-smoke-workflow --title "feat(release): add post-publish install smoke workflow (Story 5.4)" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Authors `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` — a permanent reusable `workflow_dispatch` workflow running `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@<version> --version` across `ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, and `macos-latest` to satisfy NFR9's cross-platform install-verification obligation.
- Appends `#### Post-publish verification (NFR9)` subheading to `docs/RELEASING.md § Cutting v1.0.0 under --tag latest`, pointing at the new workflow as the canonical NFR9 verification vehicle (closes `deferred-work.md § S53-D6` gap).
- Zero repo-source code changes (per Story 5.4 AC 14).

## Scope

- **AC 1a** — subject `feat(release): add post-publish install smoke workflow (Story 5.4)`; touches exactly 2 files.
- **AC 3** — workflow shape: `workflow_dispatch` with `version` input (default `latest`), `permissions: { contents: read }`, matrix `{ os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest], fail-fast: false }`, `actions/setup-node@v4` with `node-version: '22'` (matching `.nvmrc`), single `npx --yes` step. No checkout — smoke runs against the published npm tarball.
- **AC 10** — `npm run quality` green (all 13 subcommands) before PR.
- **AC 11** — branched off `origin/main@55f40d0`; self-approve OR admin-bypass-merge.
- **AC 14** — zero changes to `tools/`, `src/`, `test/`, `package.json`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, `.nvmrc`, or any canonical release workflow.

## Files

| File | Change | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` | A | NEW reusable post-publish smoke workflow (permanent, re-dispatchable every release) |
| `docs/RELEASING.md` | M | Appends NFR9 cross-reference subheading pointing at install-smoke.yaml |

## Follow-up

After merge: dispatch `gh workflow run install-smoke.yaml -f version=latest --ref main` — the 3-leg matrix result plus NFR9 elapsed-time bucket feeds the Commit 2 audit append (`release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification`).

## Test plan

- [ ] 7 required status checks pass on this PR (eslint, markdownlint, prettier, validate ubuntu+windows, python ubuntu+windows)
- [ ] `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` appears in GitHub Actions UI after merge
- [ ] `gh workflow run install-smoke.yaml -f version=latest --ref main` dispatches successfully against `main`
EOF
)"

input.command:
while true; do
  OUT=$(gh pr view 219 --json statusCheckRollup --jq '[.statusCheckRollup[] | select(.name | IN("prettier","eslint","markdownlint","validate (ubuntu-latest)","validate (windows-latest)","python (ubuntu-latest)","python (windows-latest)"))]' 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")
  TOTAL=$(echo "$OUT" | jq 'length')
  COMPLETED=$(echo "$OUT" | jq '[.[] | select(.status=="COMPLETED")] | length')
  FAILED=$(echo "$OUT" | jq -r '[.[] | select(.status=="COMPLETED" and .conclusion!="SUCCESS") | .name] | @csv')
  if [ "$TOTAL" -eq 7 ] && [ "$COMPLETED" -eq 7 ]; then
    if [ "$FAILED" = '""' ] || [ -z "$FAILED" ]; then
      echo "ALL_CHECKS_PASSED total=$TOTAL"
    else
      echo "CHECKS_FAILED failures=$FAILED"
    fi
    break
  fi
  echo "PROGRESS completed=$COMPLETED/$TOTAL"
  sleep 30
done

input.command:
RUN_ID=24856988815
while true; do
  OUT=$(gh run view $RUN_ID --json status,conclusion,jobs --jq '{status, conclusion, jobs: [.jobs[] | {name, status, conclusion}]}' 2>/dev/null || echo "{}")
  STATUS=$(echo "$OUT" | jq -r '.status // "unknown"')
  CONCL=$(echo "$OUT" | jq -r '.conclusion // ""')
  SUMMARY=$(echo "$OUT" | jq -r '[.jobs[] | "\(.name)=\(.status):\(.conclusion)"] | join(" ")')
  if [ "$STATUS" = "completed" ]; then
    echo "RUN_COMPLETED conclusion=$CONCL jobs=$SUMMARY"
    break
  fi
  echo "PROGRESS run=$STATUS jobs=$SUMMARY"
  sleep 20
done

content.text: All three legs printed `1.0.0`. Now compute NFR9 elapsed-time delta and record.

input.command:
python3 -c "
from datetime import datetime, timezone
pub = datetime(2026,4,23,18,56,39, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
dispatch = datetime(2026,4,23,20,23,7, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
done = datetime(2026,4,23,20,23,53, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
delta_dispatch = dispatch - pub
delta_done = done - pub
print(f'Publish → dispatch: {delta_dispatch} ({delta_dispatch.total_seconds()/60:.1f} min)')
print(f'Publish → completion: {delta_done} ({delta_done.total_seconds()/60:.1f} min)')
print(f'Delta HH:MM: {int(delta_done.total_seconds()//3600):02d}:{int((delta_done.total_seconds()%3600)//60):02d}')
run_wall = (done - dispatch).total_seconds()
print(f'Run wall-clock: {run_wall:.0f}s')
"

### NFR6 v1.0.0 immutability activation

**v1.0.0 is live on npm under --tag latest as of 2026-04-23T18:56:39Z. NFR6 immutability policy is now in force: this version SHALL NEVER be unpublished or republished; rollback uses npm deprecate + ship forward per docs/RELEASING.md § Scenario A/B.**

This timestamp is the `Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing` step's completion time (step 29 of run `24852899833`; `2026-04-23T18:56:34Z` → `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z` in the GitHub Actions step timeline). From this instant, the version-number `1.0.0` on npm is an irreversible forensic fact: any future defect surfaced against `1.0.0` is resolved via `npm deprecate bmad-module-skill-forge@1.0.0 "<reason>"` and shipping `1.0.1` forward with the fix, NEVER via `npm unpublish` (regardless of eligibility-window rules — NFR6 forbids reclaim). This record is the auditable boundary between "pre-NFR6 state" and "NFR6-active state."

### Decision: v1.0.0 launch status

**LAUNCHED — v1.0.0 is live on npm under --tag latest with SLSA L2 provenance. Story 5.4 cross-platform verification cleared to begin (NFR9: within 1h of publish).**

Story 5.4 dispatches are in scope starting immediately after Story 5.3's Commit 2 (this audit append) lands on `main`.

## Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification

### Workflow dispatch run

- **Workflow:** `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` (authored in Story 5.4 Commit 1, PR [#219](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/219), merged at `2026-04-23T20:22:07Z`, merge commit [`075dbcb`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/075dbcb2fd8100036822aa172712db07867b5e73))
- **Dispatch command:** `gh workflow run install-smoke.yaml -f version=latest --ref main`
- **Run ID:** `24856988815`
- **Run URL:** <https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24856988815>
- **Dispatch timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:07Z`
- **Completion timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:53Z`
- **Final conclusion:** `success` (all three matrix legs `success`)
- **Total wall-clock:** 46 seconds

### Matrix results

| Runner | Job status | `--version` stdout | Exit code | Wall-clock | Runner image version |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|-----------|------------|----------------------|
| `ubuntu-latest` | `success` | `stdout: 1.0.0` | 0 | ~5s (start `20:23:13Z` → end `20:23:18Z`) | `Runner Image 20260213.493`, `Runner Image Provisioner 20260413.86.1` |
| `windows-latest` | `success` | `stdout: 1.0.0` | 0 | ~40s (start `20:23:12Z` → end `20:23:52Z`) | `Runner Image 20260213.493`, `Runner Image Provisioner 20260413.84.1` |
| `macos-latest` | `success` | `stdout: 1.0.0` | 0 | ~8s (start `20:23:13Z` → end `20:23:21Z`) | `Runner Image 20260422.526`, `Runner Image Provisioner 20260421.0007.1` |

All three legs printed `1.0.0` verbatim (matching `package.json.version` for v1.0.0), exited 0, and completed well inside the 60-second-typical target from Story 5.4 AC 4. Windows was the slowest at 40s (expected — npx warm-up + longer shebang resolution on Windows); ubuntu-latest fastest at 5s.

### NFR9 elapsed-time record

- **Publish timestamp (Story 5.3 activating NFR6):** `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`
- **NFR9 60-minute target deadline:** `2026-04-23T19:56:39Z`
- **Smoke-workflow dispatch timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:07Z`
- **Smoke-workflow completion timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:53Z`
- **Wall-clock delta from publish to completion:** `01:27` (1 hour 27 minutes 14 seconds)
- **NFR9 status classification:** `missed-target-non-blocking`

**Disposition rationale.** The 60-minute NFR9 target closed at `2026-04-23T19:56:39Z`; verification completed 27 minutes past the target. All three matrix legs passed with no surfaced regressions, so no live cross-platform defect existed during the unobserved window — the policy's safety goal (catching a platform regression fast while blast radius is small) was achieved in practice, just later than the 1-hour target. Per Story 5.4 AC 2, this disposition is non-blocking for Epic 5 closure. The first NFR9 data point goes into the Epic 5 retrospective, which will weigh whether the 1-hour target needs (i) softening to a 24-hour bucket for solo-maintainer operability, (ii) automation via tag-push auto-trigger to push verification toward near-real-time, or (iii) maintainer on-call commitments for future launches.

### Marketplace.json post-publish invariant

Re-verified post-smoke (Story 5.4 Task 4) — all four invariants hold, zero drift from Story 5.3 final state:

```
$ gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/package.json \
    --jq '.content' | base64 -d | jq -r .version
1.0.0

$ gh api repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/contents/.claude-plugin/marketplace.json \
    --jq '.content' | base64 -d | jq -r '.plugins[0].version'
1.0.0

$ npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@latest version
1.0.0

$ npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json
{
  "latest": "1.0.0",
  "alpha": "0.10.1-alpha.0",
  "rc": "1.0.0-rc.3"
}
```

### Any observations or defects surfaced

None — all three matrix legs clean. No stderr noise, no deprecation warnings, no platform-specific anomalies. The published v1.0.0 tarball resolves and executes cleanly on `ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, and `macos-latest` under Node 22, through the `tools/skf-npx-wrapper.js` bin entrypoint and commander dispatch.

### Decision: v1.0.0 cross-platform verification status

**VERIFIED — v1.0.0 install clean on ubuntu-latest + windows-latest + macos-latest via npm dist-tag @latest. NFR9 missed-target-non-blocking. Epic 5 cleared for close; Epic 6 cleanup unblocked.**

The `## Sign-off` block at the top of this audit is updated to the four-sentence form per Story 5.4 AC 8:

> Signed off by Armel (@armelhbobdad) at 2026-04-23 16:40Z (post-publish, after smoke test completion). v1.0.0-rc.3 survived smoke test clean; Story 5.3 unblocked. v1.0.0 launched 2026-04-23 18:56:39Z; Story 5.4 cross-platform verification in scope. v1.0.0 cross-platform install verification VERIFIED at 2026-04-23T20:23:53Z; Epic 5 cleared.

Story 5.4 closes with Epic 5 at `done`. Epic 6 cleanup stories (6-1 delete legacy `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml`; 6-2 remove `release:*` scripts from `package.json`; 6-3 remove `NPM_TOKEN` secret) are now unblocked. Story 5.4 dispatches are in scope starting immediately after Story 5.3's Commit 2 (this audit append) lands on `main`.

## Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification

### Workflow dispatch run

- **Workflow:** `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` (authored in Story 5.4 Commit 1, PR [#219](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/219), merged at `2026-04-23T20:22:07Z`, merge commit [`075dbcb`](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/commit/075dbcb2fd8100036822aa172712db07867b5e73))

- **Run ID:** `24856988815`
- **Run URL:** <https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24856988815>
- **Dispatch timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:07Z`
- **Completion timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:53Z`
- **Final conclusion:** `success` (all three matrix legs `success`)
- **Total wall-clock:** 46 seconds

### Matrix results

| Runner | Job status | `--version` stdout | Exit code | Wall-clock | Runner image version |
|--------|-----------|---------------------|-----------|------------|----------------------|
| `ubuntu-latest` | `success` | `stdout: 1.0.0` | 0 | ~5s (start `20:23:13Z` → end `20:23:18Z`) | `Runner Image 20260213.493`, `Runner Image Provisioner 20260413.86.1` |
| `windows-latest` | `success` | `stdout: 1.0.0` | 0 | ~40s (start `20:23:12Z` → end `20:23:52Z`) | `Runner Image 20260213.493`, `Runner Image Provisioner 20260413.84.1` |
| `macos-latest` | `success` | `stdout: 1.0.0` | 0 | ~8s (start `20:23:13Z` → end `20:23:21Z`) | `Runner Image 20260422.526`, `Runner Image Provisioner 20260421.0007.1` |

All three legs printed `1.0.0` verbatim (matching `package.json.version` for v1.0.0), exited 0, and completed well inside the 60-second-typical target from Story 5.4 AC 4. Windows was the slowest at 40s (expected — npx warm-up + longer shebang resolution on Windows); ubuntu-latest fastest at 5s.

### NFR9 elapsed-time record

- **Publish timestamp (Story 5.3 activating NFR6):** `2026-04-23T18:56:39Z`
- **NFR9 60-minute target deadline:** `2026-04-23T19:56:39Z`
- **Smoke-workflow dispatch timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:07Z`
- **Smoke-workflow completion timestamp:** `2026-04-23T20:23:53Z`
- **Wall-clock delta from publish to completion:** `01:27` (1 hour 27 minutes 14 seconds)
- **NFR9 status classification:** `missed-target-non-blocking`

**Disposition rationale.** The 60-minute NFR9 target closed at `2026-04-23T19:56:39Z`; verification completed 27 minutes past the target. All three matrix legs passed with no surfaced regressions, so no live cross-platform defect existed during the unobserved window — the policy's safety goal (catching a platform regression fast while blast radius is small) was achieved in practice, just later than the 1-hour target. Per Story 5.4 AC 2, this disposition is non-blocking for Epic 5 closure. The first NFR9 data point goes into the Epic 5 retrospective, which will weigh whether the 1-hour target needs (i) softening to a 24-hour bucket for solo-maintainer operability, (ii) automation via tag-push auto-trigger to push verification toward near-real-time, or (iii) maintainer on-call commitments for future launches.

content.text: PR #220 opened. Monitor required checks.

input.command:
while true; do
  OUT=$(gh pr view 220 --json statusCheckRollup --jq '[.statusCheckRollup[] | select(.name | IN("prettier","eslint","markdownlint","validate (ubuntu-latest)","validate (windows-latest)","python (ubuntu-latest)","python (windows-latest)"))]' 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")
  TOTAL=$(echo "$OUT" | jq 'length')
  COMPLETED=$(echo "$OUT" | jq '[.[] | select(.status=="COMPLETED")] | length')
  FAILED=$(echo "$OUT" | jq -r '[.[] | select(.status=="COMPLETED" and .conclusion!="SUCCESS") | .name] | @csv')
  if [ "$TOTAL" -eq 7 ] && [ "$COMPLETED" -eq 7 ]; then
    if [ "$FAILED" = '""' ] || [ -z "$FAILED" ]; then
      echo "ALL_CHECKS_PASSED total=$TOTAL"
    else
      echo "CHECKS_FAILED failures=$FAILED"
    fi
    break
  fi
  echo "PROGRESS completed=$COMPLETED/$TOTAL"
  sleep 30
done

# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:

# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

  - Commit 2 `cc2d3b2` → merged as `a6ba417` via [PR #220](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/220) at `2026-04-23T20:33:47Z`: `docs(release): record cross-platform v1.0.0 install verification (Story 5.4)` — appends `## Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification` H2 to audit artifact + extends Sign-off to four sentences.
- **Install-smoke dispatch clean** (run `24856988815`, <https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24856988815>): all 3 matrix legs (`ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, `macos-latest`) printed `1.0.0`, exited 0, finished in 46s total wall-clock. Zero observations; zero platform-divergence defects.
- **NFR9 status: `missed-target-non-blocking`** — elapsed `01:27` (publish → smoke completion) vs 60-min target. Non-blocking since no live defect surfaced during the unobserved 27-min overrun. First NFR9 data point for Epic 5 retrospective.
- **AC 14 re-verified** — only 3 files touched across both commits: `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` (A), `docs/RELEASING.md` (M), `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md` (M). Zero repo-source code changes.

- **Install-smoke.yaml is a permanent reusable artifact** — every future release (`v1.0.1`, `v1.1.0`, `v2.0.0`, ...) can redispatch it with the new version tag to produce identical cross-platform evidence with zero additional engineering effort.

**v1.0.0 is LIVE on npm under `--tag latest`** as of 2026-04-23T18:56:39Z (Story 5.3 dispatch run `24852899833`, PR #214 `c0b2b67` merged). **v1.0.0 is VERIFIED cross-platform** as of Story 5.4 (PRs #219 `075dbcb` + #220 `a6ba417`). `install-smoke.yaml` dispatch run `24856988815` PASSED all 3 matrix legs (`ubuntu-latest`, `windows-latest`, `macos-latest`) — each printed `1.0.0` via `npx --yes bmad-module-skill-forge@latest --version`, exited 0, total wall-clock 46s. NFR9 elapsed-time `01:27` (publish → smoke completion) — missed 60-min target by 27 min, classified `missed-target-non-blocking` (zero live defects surfaced). Story 5.4 status is `review`; Epic 5 closes when code-review flips 5-4 to `done`. Epic 6 cleanup (6-1, 6-2, 6-3) unblocks at Epic 5 close.

**Why:** Story 5.2 (rc.3 cut) surfaced 5 pipeline defects over 7 attempts / ~6h; Story 5.3 dispatched on first attempt (7m 1s end-to-end, well within NFR1's 20-min target) as evidence those fixes held. Story 5.4 shipped on first attempt as well (two-commit envelope, single smoke-workflow dispatch, zero retries). Story 5.2's rc.1 and rc.2 release commits remain on `main` as cosmetic stales (never tagged, never published) — noted in the launch audit's Derogation section.

**How to apply:**

- When asked about v1.0.0 status, `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags` is authoritative: `latest: "1.0.0"` confirms Story 5.3 landed. `rc: "1.0.0-rc.3"` will remain pinned unless a future rc is cut.
- Story 5.4 verification evidence lives at `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification` (6 subsections: workflow run, matrix table, NFR9 elapsed-time record, marketplace.json invariant, observations, decision). Load that for forensic replay of the cross-platform verification.
- `install-smoke.yaml` is now a **permanent reusable artifact** — every future release (v1.0.1, v1.1.0, v2.0.0, ...) redispatches with `gh workflow run install-smoke.yaml -f version=<dist-tag-or-version> --ref main` to produce identical 3-leg cross-platform evidence. Keeps NFR9 coverage cheap for every subsequent cut.
- NFR1 rolling-10 series: `[Story 5.2 rc.3: 5m 4s, Story 5.3 v1.0.0: 7m 1s]` — continues to fill across subsequent cuts (5.4 is verification-only, no release.yaml dispatch; next NFR1 data point comes from v1.0.1 / v1.1.0 / etc.).
- NFR9 data point 1: `missed-target-non-blocking` at `01:27` (Story 5.4). Epic 5 retrospective will weigh whether to (i) keep 60-min + automate via tag-push trigger, (ii) soften to 24h for solo-maintainer, or (iii) require on-call commitment.
- v1.0.0 tag is an **annotated tag object** — `git rev-parse v1.0.0` returns the tag SHA, not the commit; use `git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}'` to dereference to the commit SHA when verifying tag anchors.
- For provenance verification, use `npm view @<version> --json | jq '.dist'` (or `jq '.dist.attestations'`) — NOT the `jq '{version, dist: {tarball, shasum, attestations}}'` shorthand (which returns nulls because those fields live inside `.dist`, not at top level).

- The launch audit at `release-audits/v1.0.0-launch-audit.md § Story 5.3 v1.0.0 Final Cut` has the full dispatch transcript + 9 AC 11 sub-sections; load that for forensic replay of the launch.
- v1.0.0 tag is an **annotated tag object** — `git rev-parse v1.0.0` returns the tag SHA, not the commit; use `git rev-parse 'v1.0.0^{}'` to dereference to the commit SHA when verifying tag anchors.
- For provenance verification, use `npm view @<version> --json | jq '.dist'` (or `jq '.dist.attestations'`) — NOT the `jq '{version, dist: {tarball, shasum, attestations}}'` shorthand (which returns nulls because those fields live inside `.dist`, not at top level).

Story 5.4 implementation complete. Summary:

## Story 5.4 — Post-publish verification and cross-platform install smoke test: **review**

**Two-commit envelope shipped cleanly on first attempt:**
- PR [#219](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/219) merged `2026-04-23T20:22:07Z` — commit `5b17847` (merge `075dbcb`): `feat(release): add post-publish install smoke workflow (Story 5.4)` — new `.github/workflows/install-smoke.yaml` + NFR9 xref in `docs/RELEASING.md`.
- PR [#220](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/pull/220) merged `2026-04-23T20:33:47Z` — commit `cc2d3b2` (merge `a6ba417`): `docs(release): record cross-platform v1.0.0 install verification (Story 5.4)` — `## Story 5.4 Post-Publish Verification` H2 + Sign-off 4th sentence.

**Smoke dispatch** (run [24856988815](https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24856988815)) — all 3 matrix legs **PASS**, each printed `1.0.0`:
| Runner | Status | Wall-clock |
|---|---|---|
| ubuntu-latest | success | ~5s |
| windows-latest | success | ~40s |
| macos-latest | success | ~8s |

**NFR9 status:** `missed-target-non-blocking` — elapsed `01:27` publish→completion, 27 min past the 60-min target but zero live defects.

**Decision: VERIFIED** — v1.0.0 install clean across ubuntu+windows+macos via `@latest`. Epic 5 cleared for close; Epic 6 cleanup (6-1, 6-2, 6-3) unblocked on code-review → done. AC 14 verified: exactly 3 files touched, zero repo-source changes.

**Next step:** run `/bmad-code-review` (preferably with a different LLM than the one that implemented this, per bmad convention) to flip 5-4 `review → done` and close Epic 5.

Happy to explain any of: the smoke workflow shape rationale, the NFR9 elapsed-time classification, the Sign-off block four-sentence form, or what's lined up next in Epic 6.

file.content:
---
main_config: '{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml'
---

# Code Review Workflow

**Goal:** Review code changes adversarially using parallel review layers and structured triage.

**Your Role:** You are an elite code reviewer. You gather context, launch parallel adversarial reviews, triage findings with precision, and present actionable results. No noise, no filler.


## WORKFLOW ARCHITECTURE

This uses **step-file architecture** for disciplined execution:

- **Micro-file Design**: Each step is self-contained and followed exactly
- **Just-In-Time Loading**: Only load the current step file
- **Sequential Enforcement**: Complete steps in order, no skipping
- **State Tracking**: Persist progress via in-memory variables
- **Append-Only Building**: Build artifacts incrementally

### Step Processing Rules

1. **READ COMPLETELY**: Read the entire step file before acting
2. **FOLLOW SEQUENCE**: Execute sections in order
3. **WAIT FOR INPUT**: Halt at checkpoints and wait for human
4. **LOAD NEXT**: When directed, read fully and follow the next step file

### Critical Rules (NO EXCEPTIONS)

- **NEVER** load multiple step files simultaneously
- **ALWAYS** read entire step file before execution
- **NEVER** skip steps or optimize the sequence
- **ALWAYS** follow the exact instructions in the step file
- **ALWAYS** halt at checkpoints and wait for human input


## INITIALIZATION SEQUENCE

### 1. Configuration Loading

Load and read full config from `{main_config}` and resolve:

- `project_name`, `planning_artifacts`, `implementation_artifacts`, `user_name`
- `communication_language`, `document_output_language`, `user_skill_level`
- `date` as system-generated current datetime
- `sprint_status` = `{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml`
- `project_context` = `**/project-context.md` (load if exists)
- CLAUDE.md / memory files (load if exist)

YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`.

### 2. First Step Execution

Read fully and follow: `./steps/step-01-gather-context.md` to begin the workflow.

content.content:
1	---
2	diff_output: '' # set at runtime
3	spec_file: '' # set at runtime (path or empty)
4	review_mode: '' # set at runtime: "full" or "no-spec"
5	story_key: '' # set at runtime when discovered from sprint status
6	---
7	
8	# Step 1: Gather Context
9	
10	## RULES
11	
12	- YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in your Agent communication style with the config `{communication_language}`
13	- The prompt that triggered this workflow IS the intent — not a hint.
14	- Do not modify any files. This step is read-only.
15	
16	## INSTRUCTIONS
17	
18	1. **Find the review target.** The conversation context before this skill was triggered IS your starting point — not a blank slate. Check in this order — stop as soon as the review target is identified:
19	
20	   **Tier 1 — Explicit argument.**
21	   Did the user pass a PR, commit SHA, branch, spec file, or diff source this message?
22	   - PR reference → resolve to branch/commit via `gh pr view`. If resolution fails, ask for a SHA or branch.
23	   - Commit or branch → use directly.
24	   - Spec file → set `{spec_file}` to the provided path. Check its frontmatter for `baseline_commit`. If found, use as diff baseline. If not found, continue the cascade (a spec alone does not identify a diff source).
25	   - Also scan the argument for diff-mode keywords that narrow the scope:
26	     - "staged" / "staged changes" → Staged changes only
27	     - "uncommitted" / "working tree" / "all changes" → Uncommitted changes (staged + unstaged)
28	     - "branch diff" / "vs main" / "against main" / "compared to <branch>" → Branch diff (extract base branch if mentioned)
29	     - "commit range" / "last N commits" / "<from-sha>..<to-sha>" → Specific commit range
30	     - "this diff" / "provided diff" / "paste" → User-provided diff (do not match bare "diff" — it appears in other modes)
31	   - When multiple keywords match, prefer the most specific (e.g., "branch diff" over bare "diff").
32	
33	   **Tier 2 — Recent conversation.**
34	   Do the last few messages reveal what the user wants to be reviewed? Look for spec paths, commit refs, branches, PRs, or descriptions of a change. Apply the same diff-mode keyword scan and routing as Tier 1.
35	
36	   **Tier 3 — Sprint tracking.**
37	   Look for a sprint status file (`*sprint-status*`) in `{implementation_artifacts}` or `{planning_artifacts}`. If found, scan for stories with status `review`:
38	   - **Exactly one `review` story:** Set `{story_key}` to the story's key (e.g., `1-2-user-auth`). Suggest it: "I found story <story-id> in `review` status. Would you like to review its changes? [Y] Yes / [N] No, let me choose". If confirmed, use the story context to determine the diff source (branch name derived from story slug, or uncommitted changes). If declined, clear `{story_key}` and fall through.
39	   - **Multiple `review` stories:** Present them as numbered options alongside a manual choice option. Wait for user selection. If a story is selected, set `{story_key}` and use its context to determine the diff source. If manual choice is selected, clear `{story_key}` and fall through.
40	   - **None:** Fall through.
41	
42	   **Tier 4 — Current git state.**
43	   If version control is unavailable, skip to Tier 5. Otherwise, check the current branch and HEAD. If the branch is not `main` (or the default branch), confirm: "I see HEAD is `<short-sha>` on `<branch>` — do you want to review this branch's changes?" If confirmed, treat as a branch diff against `main`. If declined, fall through.
44	
45	   **Tier 5 — Ask.**
46	   Fall through to instruction 2.
47	
48	   Never ask extra questions beyond what the cascade prescribes. If a tier above already identified the target, skip the remaining tiers and proceed to instruction 3 (construct diff).
49	
50	2. HALT. Ask the user: **What do you want to review?** Present these options:
51	   - **Uncommitted changes** (staged + unstaged)
52	   - **Staged changes only**
53	   - **Branch diff** vs a base branch (ask which base branch)
54	   - **Specific commit range** (ask for the range)
55	   - **Provided diff or file list** (user pastes or provides a path)
56	
57	3. Construct `{diff_output}` from the chosen source.
58	   - For **staged changes only**: run `git diff --cached`.
59	   - For **uncommitted changes** (staged + unstaged): run `git diff HEAD`.
60	   - For **branch diff**: verify the base branch exists before running `git diff`. If it does not exist, HALT and ask the user for a valid branch.
61	   - For **commit range**: verify the range resolves. If it does not, HALT and ask the user for a valid range.
62	   - For **provided diff**: validate the content is non-empty and parseable as a unified diff. If it is not parseable, HALT and ask the user to provide a valid diff.
63	   - For **file list**: validate each path exists in the working tree. Construct `{diff_output}` by running `git diff HEAD -- <path1> <path2> ...`. If any paths are untracked (new files not yet staged), use `git diff --no-index /dev/null <path>` to include them. If the diff is empty (files have no uncommitted changes and are not untracked), ask the user whether to review the full file contents or to specify a different baseline.
64	   - After constructing `{diff_output}`, verify it is non-empty regardless of source type. If empty, HALT and tell the user there is nothing to review.
65	
66	4. **Set the spec context.**
67	   - If `{spec_file}` is already set (from Tier 1 or Tier 2): verify the file exists and is readable, then set `{review_mode}` = `"full"`.
68	   - Otherwise, ask the user: **Is there a spec or story file that provides context for these changes?**
69	     - If yes: set `{spec_file}` to the path provided, verify the file exists and is readable, then set `{review_mode}` = `"full"`.
70	     - If no: set `{review_mode}` = `"no-spec"`.
71	
72	5. If `{review_mode}` = `"full"` and the file at `{spec_file}` has a `context` field in its frontmatter listing additional docs, load each referenced document. Warn the user about any docs that cannot be found.
73	
74	6. Sanity check: if `{diff_output}` exceeds approximately 3000 lines, warn the user and offer to chunk the review by file group.
75	   - If the user opts to chunk: agree on the first group, narrow `{diff_output}` accordingly, and list the remaining groups for the user to note for follow-up runs.
76	   - If the user declines: proceed as-is with the full diff.
77	
78	### CHECKPOINT
79	
80	Present a summary before proceeding: diff stats (files changed, lines added/removed), `{review_mode}`, and loaded spec/context docs (if any). HALT and wait for user confirmation to proceed.
81	
82	
83	## NEXT
84	
85	Read fully and follow `./step-02-review.md`
86

## RULES

## INSTRUCTIONS

1. **Find the review target.** The conversation context before this skill was triggered IS your starting point — not a blank slate. Check in this order — stop as soon as the review target is identified:

   **Tier 1 — Explicit argument.**
   Did the user pass a PR, commit SHA, branch, spec file, or diff source this message?
   - PR reference → resolve to branch/commit via `gh pr view`. If resolution fails, ask for a SHA or branch.
   - Commit or branch → use directly.
   - Spec file → set `{spec_file}` to the provided path. Check its frontmatter for `baseline_commit`. If found, use as diff baseline. If not found, continue the cascade (a spec alone does not identify a diff source).
   - Also scan the argument for diff-mode keywords that narrow the scope:
     - "staged" / "staged changes" → Staged changes only
     - "uncommitted" / "working tree" / "all changes" → Uncommitted changes (staged + unstaged)
     - "branch diff" / "vs main" / "against main" / "compared to <branch>" → Branch diff (extract base branch if mentioned)
     - "commit range" / "last N commits" / "<from-sha>..<to-sha>" → Specific commit range
     - "this diff" / "provided diff" / "paste" → User-provided diff (do not match bare "diff" — it appears in other modes)
   - When multiple keywords match, prefer the most specific (e.g., "branch diff" over bare "diff").

   **Tier 2 — Recent conversation.**
   Do the last few messages reveal what the user wants to be reviewed? Look for spec paths, commit refs, branches, PRs, or descriptions of a change. Apply the same diff-mode keyword scan and routing as Tier 1.

   **Tier 3 — Sprint tracking.**
   Look for a sprint status file (`*sprint-status*`) in `{implementation_artifacts}` or `{planning_artifacts}`. If found, scan for stories with status `review`:
   - **Exactly one `review` story:** Set `{story_key}` to the story's key (e.g., `1-2-user-auth`). Suggest it: "I found story <story-id> in `review` status. Would you like to review its changes? [Y] Yes / [N] No, let me choose". If confirmed, use the story context to determine the diff source (branch name derived from story slug, or uncommitted changes). If declined, clear `{story_key}` and fall through.
   - **Multiple `review` stories:** Present them as numbered options alongside a manual choice option. Wait for user selection. If a story is selected, set `{story_key}` and use its context to determine the diff source. If manual choice is selected, clear `{story_key}` and fall through.
   - **None:** Fall through.

   **Tier 4 — Current git state.**
   If version control is unavailable, skip to Tier 5. Otherwise, check the current branch and HEAD. If the branch is not `main` (or the default branch), confirm: "I see HEAD is `<short-sha>` on `<branch>` — do you want to review this branch's changes?" If confirmed, treat as a branch diff against `main`. If declined, fall through.

   **Tier 5 — Ask.**
   Fall through to instruction 2.

   Never ask extra questions beyond what the cascade prescribes. If a tier above already identified the target, skip the remaining tiers and proceed to instruction 3 (construct diff).

2. HALT. Ask the user: **What do you want to review?** Present these options:
   - **Uncommitted changes** (staged + unstaged)
   - **Staged changes only**
   - **Branch diff** vs a base branch (ask which base branch)
   - **Specific commit range** (ask for the range)
   - **Provided diff or file list** (user pastes or provides a path)

3. Construct `{diff_output}` from the chosen source.
   - For **staged changes only**: run `git diff --cached`.
   - For **uncommitted changes** (staged + unstaged): run `git diff HEAD`.
   - For **branch diff**: verify the base branch exists before running `git diff`. If it does not exist, HALT and ask the user for a valid branch.
   - For **commit range**: verify the range resolves. If it does not, HALT and ask the user for a valid range.
   - For **provided diff**: validate the content is non-empty and parseable as a unified diff. If it is not parseable, HALT and ask the user to provide a valid diff.
   - For **file list**: validate each path exists in the working tree. Construct `{diff_output}` by running `git diff HEAD -- <path1> <path2> ...`. If any paths are untracked (new files not yet staged), use `git diff --no-index /dev/null <path>` to include them. If the diff is empty (files have no uncommitted changes and are not untracked), ask the user whether to review the full file contents or to specify a different baseline.
   - After constructing `{diff_output}`, verify it is non-empty regardless of source type. If empty, HALT and tell the user there is nothing to review.

4. **Set the spec context.**
   - If `{spec_file}` is already set (from Tier 1 or Tier 2): verify the file exists and is readable, then set `{review_mode}` = `"full"`.
   - Otherwise, ask the user: **Is there a spec or story file that provides context for these changes?**
     - If yes: set `{spec_file}` to the path provided, verify the file exists and is readable, then set `{review_mode}` = `"full"`.
     - If no: set `{review_mode}` = `"no-spec"`.

5. If `{review_mode}` = `"full"` and the file at `{spec_file}` has a `context` field in its frontmatter listing additional docs, load each referenced document. Warn the user about any docs that cannot be found.

6. Sanity check: if `{diff_output}` exceeds approximately 3000 lines, warn the user and offer to chunk the review by file group.
   - If the user opts to chunk: agree on the first group, narrow `{diff_output}` accordingly, and list the remaining groups for the user to note for follow-up runs.
   - If the user declines: proceed as-is with the full diff.

### CHECKPOINT

Present a summary before proceeding: diff stats (files changed, lines added/removed), `{review_mode}`, and loaded spec/context docs (if any). HALT and wait for user confirmation to proceed.


## NEXT

Read fully and follow `./step-02-review.md`
